results.title: Grass and conservation
results.brief_description: Grass is indirectly involved in the production of many essentials commodities - butter, milk, meat, clothes, blankets and soap. Therefore its incorrect use could upset the present and future prosperity of the country. Methods of grass conservation include prevention of overgrazing, extermination of rabbits, and other pests, planting of grass and trees on damaged land and the instruction of young farmers in conservation practices.

results.title: Graphs correlation regression elasticity (TEFC)
results.brief_description: Deals with a study of the principles by which resources are allocated and production organised. Andrew Pollard examines a number of statistical tools frequently used by economists. These include the graph, regression and multiple regression

results.title: Grapevine pruning
results.brief_description: The method of pruning sultana grapes developed as a result of research on vine buds and shoots, is shown. The correct methods of pruning Zante currants, Gordos, and other varieties of grapes is also shown. The research work was carried out by CSIRO at the Commonwealth Research Station, Merbein, Victoria.

results.title: Granville
results.brief_description: Depicts the aftermath of the railway disaster at Granville, New South Wales in February 1977. Eighty-two persons were killed and over eighty were injured. The film shows the rescue operations in action. Suitable for secondary level.

results.title: Grant Wood 1892-1942 (TEFC)
results.brief_description: Presents the story of Grant Wood, an American self-taught artist who painted the gentle curves of his neighbouring hills, the neatly ploughed fields, the daily routine of western life as well as such classic themes in American History as Paul Revere’s ride. The film brings together a collection of Grant Wood’s best work. Credits include: photography by John Lewis; music by Lan Adomian. Narrated by Henry Fonda.

results.title: The Grand design
results.creator_credit: United Nations. Dept. of Public Information
results.brief_description: Shows the beginnings of the United Nations Organization, its political work for peace, and its achievements and aims in the fields of agriculture, health, and famine control Perhaps most inspiring are the sections dealing with economic aid and health projects which show how wide is the UN’s scope and effectiveness it these fields.

results.title: Grand Central Market
results.creator_credit: William Hale and United States Information Agency
results.brief_description: In the city of Los Angeles, California, is a large central food market catering for the needs of its large population. This film shows the mixed population of the area shopping in an atmosphere of freedom and friendship.

results.title: The Grain that built a hemisphere
results.brief_description: A study of the part played by maize in the development of both the past and the present civilizations of the American continents. The history of the Americas is graphically presented in terms of the economic and sociological factors which determined the lives of the peoples who inhabited these lands.

results.title: A Grain of sand
results.creator_credit: David Keith Hardy, John Luck and UNICEF
results.brief_description: Firstly children’s needs are dramatised through a poem by William Blake and drawings by Ronald Searle. Then we follow a 10 year old Tunisian Arab boy to a health clinic, a school and a vocational training centre - three concrete ways to help meet children’s needs.

results.title: Graham Sutherland
results.creator_credit: John Read, John Elliot and BBC-TV
results.brief_description: A films about distinguished English painter best known for his portraits of Somerset Maugham, Lord Beaverbrook and Sir Winston Churchill. Sutherland is, however, a landscape artist who treats his subjects in a highly personal and poetic manner, which goes beyond surface realism. Descriptive sequences were specially shot in Pembrokeshire and in the South of France to show landscapes which have influenced his painting. The film concludes with a personal statement by the artist about his approach to his work.
